body:
## Summary

The current `scripts/check-file-size.rs` enforces a hard `MAX_LINES = 1000` limit for Rust files, but it has no warning band for files that are close to the limit. This creates the same concurrent-PR merge race documented in the JavaScript template issue:

- https://github.com/link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/issues/41
- Downstream case study: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/tree/main/docs/case-studies/issue-1593

Two PRs can each pass CI when tested independently, then merge close together and push the combined file over the limit. The first hard failure is then the post-merge `main` CI run, which can gate downstream release/build jobs.

## Reproduction

1. Start with a Rust file near the limit, for example 999 lines.
2. Open PR-A that adds 1 line. PR-A remains at 1000 lines and passes `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`.
3. Open PR-B from the same base that adds 1 line. PR-B also remains at 1000 lines and passes independently.
4. Merge PR-A, then merge PR-B without rerunning PR-B against PR-A's merged result.
5. The first `main` CI run after PR-B sees 1001 lines and fails the file-size gate.

## Suggested Fix

Add a warn-only threshold below the hard limit, analogous to the JavaScript template's `WARN_THRESHOLD=1350` for a 1500-line limit. For this Rust template, a 10% buffer would be:

```rust
const MAX_LINES: usize = 1000;
const WARN_LINES: usize = 900;
```

Suggested behavior:

- Keep the existing hard failure when `line_count > MAX_LINES`.
- Emit a GitHub Actions `::warning file=...::...` annotation when `line_count > WARN_LINES` and `line_count <= MAX_LINES`.
- Print a warning summary listing files in the warn band.
- Keep the warning non-blocking so existing PRs are not broken, but reviewers get an early signal to split or refactor large files before concurrent merges can cross the hard limit.

## Workaround

Before the template is updated, contributors can manually run:

```bash
find . -name '*.rs' -type f -not -path '*/target/*' -exec wc -l {} + | sort -n
```

and proactively split files above about 900 lines.
title: check-file-size.rs: add warn threshold to mitigate concurrent PR merge race
